description: Spark drops Scala 2.12, moving exclusively to Scala 2.13 as the default. Additionally, the Scala Connect client now has full API parity with the classic Dataset/DataFrame API.
Introduction of the native VARIANT data type to support highly flexible, semi-structured data operations natively in Spark. Used with PARSE_JSON to store schema-less JSON efficiently.

Writing multi-step SQL workflows is easier in Spark 4.0 thanks to new SQL scripting capabilities. Execute multi-statement SQL scripts with local variables (DECLARE, SET VAR) and control flow (IF/ELSE, WHILE loops), completely replacing the need for many legacy stored procedures.
Added Catalog APIs for loading stored procedures, expanding DSv2 capabilities to manage procedural execution externally.
Introduction of a fully built-in PySpark Plotting API, supporting KDE, density, line, bar, and histogram plots natively via Plotly backends.

Fully built-in support for reading and writing XML files via spark.read.format('xml') and spark.write.format('xml'), eliminating third-party libraries. Functions like schema_of_xml and from_xml are natively integrated.
Redesign of the state management API allowing multiple state variables, Map states, and flexible column families natively in the state store for complex event processing.
